steps.title: Find the consequence
steps.content: The first sentence identifies a limitation of the Curiosity rover: it could not preserve samples for later analysis. The second sentence says the Perseverance team tried to build a mechanism that could preserve samples, so the transition should mark a result.

steps.title: Reject time and similarity cues
steps.content: Choice A wrongly places the action earlier. Choice B would introduce a specific detail of the same idea, but the second sentence gives a response to the limitation. Choice C suggests similarity, though Perseverance was designed to do what Curiosity could not. The answer is D.
